content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be structured as hierarchical Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) Reward Machines, where the agent’s active selection of new memory trajectories to encode is rewarded for filling gaps in the temporal logic constraints of long-term task knowledge. This framework turns passive memory storage into an curiosity-driven active learning process that rewards agents for discovering un-verified temporal dependencies in their stored memories.
